title: spergularia manicata
intro: Spergularia manicata, also known as creeping sandwort, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the caryophyllaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Spergularia manicata include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Spergularia manicata can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
